pages.text: The $(item)Trade Post Skyseed$() — $(item)2 Hamlet Skyseeds$(), dirt and a little timber. A little street village of shops, several trades in one throw.$(br2)$(bold)Where to look$()$(br)Craft up two Hamlets first (each wants an emerald off a $(item)Rocky$() isle), then fold them into a Trade Post.
Everything's in hand — here's how it goes together.
A small street village — a central square radiating lanes lined with shops, fields and gardens, each shop with its own villager. The mid-tier village island: one throw, $(bold)2 to 4$() trades.

$(bold)The shops$()$(br)Each cottage comes with a $(item)job-site block$() and a bed, so its villager takes up that trade and settles in — farmer, librarian, fisherman, fletcher. Larger lots may host a landmark: a blacksmith's $(item)forge$() or a meeting hall. Which you get is the luck of the throw, and they're styled to the biome.

$(bold)Assembled by jigsaw$()$(br)The square and its streets are pieced together like a real village — shops on the lots, fields and gardens between, bridges out over the void — so no two trade posts share a layout. Costs $(item)2 Hamlet Skyseeds$(), dirt and timber — two cottages grown into a whole street.
